mission.title: seaweedfs: Leader election doesn't seem to be working on 4.10
mission.description: Leader election doesn't seem to be working on 4.10. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current seaweedfs deployment
steps.description:
Verify your seaweedfs version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
helm list -n seaweedfs 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working seaweedfs installation.

steps.title: Review seaweedfs configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant seaweedfs configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
kubectl get configmap -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=seaweedfs
```
Tried upgraded from 4.09 to 4.10 today and I can't seem to get a leader elected. Reverting to 4.09 works with the same setup. Then upgrading again fails.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Leader election doesn't seem to be working on 4.10
steps.description:
## Summary
- normalize the hashicorp Raft voter IDs to the HTTP address so bootstrap sees the local node as a voter
- keep gRPC transport running on the grpc port while using the normalized IDs for the configuration and peer updates
- add unit tests to cover canonicalization logic

## Testing
- go
```yaml
$ kubectl exec -it -n seaweedfs weed-master-0 -- weed shell
master: localhost:9333 filer: 
I0209 09:08:28.749594 masterclient.go:385 .adminShell masterClient bootstraps with masters {[localhost:9333] <nil>}
I0209 09:08:28.757902 masterclient.go:179 .adminShell masterClient gRPC stream established to localhost:9333 in 4.669206ms
I0209 09:08:38.823395 masterclient.go:370 .adminShell WaitUntilConnected still waiting for master connection (attempt 100)...
I0209 09:08:48.336459 masterclient.go:370 .adminShell WaitUntilConnected still waiting for master connection (attempt 200)...
I0209 09:08:58.445
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
kubectl get events -n seaweedfs --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Leader election doesn't seem to be working on 4.10" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Summary
- normalize the hashicorp Raft voter IDs to the HTTP address so bootstrap sees the local node as a voter
- keep gRPC transport running on the grpc port while using the normalized IDs for the configuration and peer updates
- add unit tests to cover canonicalization logic

## Testing
- go test ./weed/server ./weed/command -count=1

## Summary by CodeRabbit

*

* Updated server peer

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with seaweedfs installed or the issue environment reproducible.
